question: In a scratch-card competition, 945 cards were printed and 210 contain a winning prize. If you buy one card, what is the probability that you DO NOT win a prize? (Give as a percentage, rounded to 1 decimal place).

Data: Boys taking Math= 20, Boys taking Math Lit= 12, Girls taking Math= 18, Girls taking Math Lit= 12. A learner is selected at random and it is known that they take Math. What is the probability that this learner is a Girl? (Give as a decimal rounded to 2 decimal places).
explanation: This is conditional probability. Total taking Math = 20 + 18 = 38. Number of Girls taking Math = 18. P(Girl | Math) = 18 / 38 = 0.47.

question: Data: Boys taking Math= 14, Boys taking Math Lit= 10, Girls taking Math= 14, Girls taking Math Lit= 19. A learner is selected at random and it is known that they take Math. What is the probability that this learner is a Girl? (Give as a decimal rounded to 2 decimal places).
explanation: This is conditional probability. Total taking Math = 14 + 14 = 28. Number of Girls taking Math = 14. P(Girl | Math) = 14 / 28 = 0.50.

Cylindrical cans of radius 4 cm and height 24 cm are to be packed upright into a rectangular box of dimensions 32 cm × 24 cm × 24 cm. How many cans will fit into the box?
explanation: Diameter of can = 4 × 2 = 8 cm. Fit lengthways = 32 ÷ 8 = 4. Fit widthways = 24 ÷ 8 = 3. Total cans = 4 × 3 = 12.

question: Mpho wants to paint the outside of a closed rectangular floor (including top and bottom) with dimensions 5m × 6m × 4m. Calculate the total surface area to be painted. (Give only the numerical value in m²).
A cylindrical box has a radius of 5m and a height of 9m. Calculate its volume. (Use π ≈ 3.142 and round to 2 decimal places).
explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = 3.142 × 5² × 9 = 706.95 m³.

question: A circular floor has a radius of 6m. Calculate the circumference. (Use π ≈ 3.142 and round to 2 decimal places).
Cylindrical cans of radius 3 cm and height 23 cm are to be packed upright into a rectangular box of dimensions 12 cm × 24 cm × 23 cm. How many cans will fit into the box?
explanation: Diameter of can = 3 × 2 = 6 cm. Fit lengthways = 12 ÷ 6 = 2. Fit widthways = 24 ÷ 6 = 4. Total cans = 2 × 4 = 8.

question: A bus departs at 06:30 and the journey takes 4 hours and 35 minutes. At what time will the bus arrive? (Format as HH:MM).
explanation: Add 4 hours and 35 minutes to 06:30. Minutes = 30 + 35 = 65. 65 mins = 1 hr and 5 mins. Hours = 6 + 4 + 1 = 11. Arrival time is 11:05.

question: Data: Boys taking Math= 16, Boys taking Math Lit= 13, Girls taking Math= 13, Girls taking Math Lit= 17. A learner is selected at random and it is known that they take Math. What is the probability that this learner is a Girl? (Give as a decimal rounded to 2 decimal places).
explanation: This is conditional probability. Total taking Math = 16 + 13 = 29. Number of Girls taking Math = 13. P(Girl | Math) = 13 / 29 = 0.45.

A bus departs at 10:15 and the journey takes 5 hours and 35 minutes. At what time will the bus arrive? (Format as HH:MM).
explanation: Add 5 hours and 35 minutes to 10:15. Minutes = 15 + 35 = 50. 50 mins = 0 hr and 50 mins. Hours = 10 + 5 + 0 = 15. Arrival time is 15:50.

explanation: This is conditional probability. Total taking Math = 10 + 18 = 28. Number of Girls taking Math = 18. P(Girl | Math) = 18 / 28 = 0.64.

question: Mandla wants to tile a rectangular room measuring 8m by 5m. If the tiles cost R366 per square meter, calculate the total cost of the tiles.
explanation: Area = 8 × 5 = 40 m². Total Cost = Area × Cost per m² = 40 × R366 = R14640.

question: Mandla wants to tile a rectangular room measuring 20m by 9m. If the tiles cost R304 per square meter, calculate the total cost of the tiles.
explanation: Area = 20 × 9 = 180 m². Total Cost = Area × Cost per m² = 180 × R304 = R54720.
